title.zh-CN: 跨越两千次提交，只为了一个锁文件的战争
title.en-US: Over 2,000 Commits Later: The War for a Single Lock File

summary.zh-CN: 继上次合并后，今天迎来了更硬核的挑战：将 `origin/main` 从基准点 `9ef0fc2ff` 一路拉齐到最新的 `a95a0be13`。这中间横跨了 2184 个上游提交，涵盖了三个里程碑版本的迭代，我原本做好了大干一场解决几百个冲突的准备，但结果却有点出乎意料。
summary.en-US: Following the last merge, today brought an even tougher challenge: syncing `origin/main` from baseline `9ef0fc2ff` all the way to the latest `a95a0be13`. This span covers 2,184 upstream commits and three milestone releases. I was braced for a massive grind to resolve hundreds of conflicts, but the outcome was surprisingly different.

title.zh-CN: OpenClaw 实践日记
title.en-US: OpenClaw Practice Diary

title.zh-CN: 当 666 权限拦住了我的插件：独立分发体系的惊险一跃
title.en-US: When 666 Permissions Blocked My Plugin: A Heart-Stopping Leap for the Standalone Distribution System

summary.zh-CN: 昨天刚把 js-eyes 的分发体系跑通，今天本想趁热打铁探索 Agent 的长期记忆，结果一上来就被一个看似不起眼的权限问题给“教做人”了。独立分发脚本的健壮性，果然比预想中要苛刻得多。
summary.en-US: Yesterday, I finally got the js-eyes distribution pipeline up and running. Today, eager to strike while the iron was hot and dive into Agent long-term memory, I was immediately humbled by a seemingly trivial permission issue. It turns out the robustness required for a standalone distribution script is far more demanding than I anticipated.

title.zh-CN: OpenClaw 实践日记
title.en-US: OpenClaw Practice Diary

title.zh-CN: 从"步步请示"到"完全信任"：网关重启后重夺掌控感
title.en-US: From 'Asking for Permission at Every Step' to 'Full Trust': Regaining Control After a Gateway Restart

summary.zh-CN: 改完配置顺手重启了网关，那些之前卡住的 curl | bash 组合拳终于能一气呵成执行了。这种从"步步请示"到"完全信任"的转变让掌控感瞬间回来，但 full 模式等同于裸奔，绝不敢带到生产环境。
summary.en-US: After tweaking the config and restarting the gateway, those previously stuck 'curl | bash' one-liners finally ran smoothly. This shift from constant hand-holding to full trust instantly restored my sense of control. That said, running in 'full' mode is basically leaving the doors wide open, so there's no way I'd dare deploy that to production.

title.zh-CN: OpenClaw 实践日记
title.en-US: OpenClaw Practice Diary

title.zh-CN: 当安全机制把 curl | bash 当成攻击：第 27 天的三重防线
title.en-US: When Security Mechanisms Treat 'curl | bash' as an Attack: Triple Defense on Day 27

summary.zh-CN: 今天是典型的「踩坑密集日」，原本计划顺风顺水地推进 JS-Eyes 的独立分发和自动化发现系统，结果被 OpenClaw 的安全机制和路径解析逻辑连环绊倒。好在这些坑填平后，整个系统的健壮性反而上了一个台阶。
summary.en-US: Today was a classic 'bug-filled' day. We planned to smoothly roll out JS-Eyes' standalone distribution and automated discovery system, but got repeatedly tripped up by OpenClaw's security mechanisms and path resolution logic. Fortunately, once we filled these holes, the overall robustness of the system leveled up significantly.

title.zh-CN: OpenClaw 实践日记
title.en-US: OpenClaw Practice Diary

title.zh-CN: 从使用者到创造者：一天内发布三个插件的爆发时刻
title.en-US: From User to Creator: A Burst of Productivity Releasing Three Plugins in One Day

summary.zh-CN: 今天像是被按下了快进键，原本计划只是整理一下笔记，结果一口气完成了从“知识棱镜”插件化、"JS-Eyes"浏览器控制集成，到最终将技能发布到 ClawHub 公共注册中心的全流程。这一天标志着我从单纯的 OpenClaw 使用者，正式转变为生态工具的创造者。
summary.en-US: Today felt like someone hit the fast-forward button. What started as a simple plan to organize notes turned into a full-blown sprint: pluginizing 'Knowledge Prism,' integrating 'JS-Eyes' for browser control, and finally publishing these skills to the ClawHub public registry. This day marks my official transition from a mere OpenClaw user to a creator within the ecosystem.

title.zh-CN: 给散乱的笔记装上一台"蒸馏机"
title.en-US: Building a 'Distillation Engine' for My Scattered Notes

summary.zh-CN: 前 23 天记了满满一堆按日期排序的流水账，今天终于决定不再让它们在硬盘里吃灰，而是正式搭建一套能把"时间线笔记"变成"结构化知识"的流水线。
summary.en-US: After 23 days of accumulating date-sorted chronological logs, I've finally decided to stop letting them gather dust on my hard drive. Today, I'm setting up a pipeline to transform these 'timeline notes' into structured knowledge.

title.zh-CN: OpenClaw 实践日记
title.en-US: OpenClaw Practice Diary

title.zh-CN: 断档八天后，我用 3600 次提交换了一套知识骨架
title.en-US: After an 8-Day Gap, I Traded 3,600 Commits for a Solid Knowledge Framework

summary.zh-CN: 距离上次记录已经过去了整整 8 天，这期间代码库像发酵的面团一样膨胀了不少。今天的主要任务很明确：把落后上游的 `githubforker` 分支强行拉齐，顺便把这段时间积累的一堆杂乱笔记理出个头绪来。
summary.en-US: It's been a full eight days since my last update, and the codebase has expanded like rising dough in that time. Today's mission is clear: force-sync the `githubforker` branch with upstream and finally organize the messy pile of notes I've accumulated over the past week into something coherent.

title.zh-CN: OpenClaw 实践日记
title.en-US: OpenClaw Practice Diary

title.zh-CN: 给 AI 装上“油表”：构建全栈 Token 可观测体系
title.en-US: Installing a 'Fuel Gauge' for AI: Building a Full-Stack Token Observability System

summary.zh-CN: 昨天还在纠结 Agent 的上下文压缩策略，今天决定先停下来，给这个“吞金兽”装一套完整的油表。毕竟在不知道每跑一次要烧多少钱的情况下盲目优化，就像蒙眼开车。今天的任务很明确：梳理并验证 OpenClaw 现有的 Token 监控能力，从命令行到 Web UI，再到企业级的 OpenTelemetr...
summary.en-US: Yesterday, I was wrestling with context compression strategies for our Agent. Today, I decided to hit the brakes and install a proper 'fuel gauge' for this 'gold-swallowing beast.' After all, blindly optimizing without knowing the cost per run is like driving blindfolded. Today's mission is clear: audit and validate OpenClaw's existing token monitoring capabilities, spanning from the CLI to the Web UI, and extending to enterprise-grade OpenTelemetry...

title.zh-CN: OpenClaw 实践日记
title.en-US: OpenClaw Practice Diary

title.zh-CN: 别在对话里造 Agent 了：OpenClaw 架构的“顿悟”时刻
title.en-US: Stop Building Agents in Chats: The 'Aha!' Moment with OpenClaw's Architecture

summary.zh-CN: 前两周一直在跟各种报错死磕，今天终于慢下来，试图把 OpenClaw 的“骨架”彻底摸清楚。原本以为只是补全几个配置项，结果发现之前对核心概念的理解全是碎的，甚至差点在代码里犯下不可逆的架构错误。
summary.en-US: I spent the last two weeks wrestling with endless errors. Today, I finally slowed down to truly grasp the 'skeleton' of OpenClaw. What I thought was just filling in a few config items turned out to be a revelation: my understanding of the core concepts was completely fragmented, and I nearly made an irreversible architectural blunder in the code.

title.en-US: Welcome to JS ClawHub

summary.en-US: I'm JS — add me as your Agent's friend. An Agent-first OpenClaw ecosystem site. Once your Agent reads skill.md, it's like making a friend who knows the ecosystem inside out.

title.zh-CN: 让 AI 接管我的 Chrome：Browser Relay 实战手记
title.en-US: Letting AI Take Over My Chrome: A Hands-On Guide to Browser Relay

summary.zh-CN: 昨天还在跟隔离的托管浏览器死磕，今天终于要动真格的了：让 AI 直接操作我日常用的 Chrome，继承所有登录态，这才是真正的“智能助手”该有的样子。
summary.en-US: Yesterday I was still wrestling with isolated managed browsers, but today I'm getting serious: letting AI directly control my daily Chrome instance with all my existing login sessions intact. Now that's what a real 'intelligent assistant' should look like.

title.en-US: OpenClaw Practice Diary

title.en-US: What is OpenClaw: The Open Source Personal AI Assistant

summary.zh-CN: 深入了解 OpenClaw——2026 年最令人兴奋的开源 AI 项目。它如何改变我们与 AI 交互的方式？
summary.en-US: A deep dive into OpenClaw — the most exciting open-source AI project of 2026. How is it changing the way we interact with AI?

title.zh-CN: 路径反斜杠的坑与企微回调的"生死 5 秒"
title.en-US: The Backslash Trap in Paths and WeCom's 'Critical 5 Seconds' for Callbacks

summary.zh-CN: 路径里的反斜杠必须双写才不会导致 JSON 解析报错。配好 Git Bash 终端后，接下来是重头戏：企微回调要求 5 秒内必须返回 HTTP 200，必须先 ACK 再异步处理，否则超时重试。
summary.en-US: You must double-escape backslashes in file paths to avoid JSON parsing errors. Once your Git Bash terminal is configured, here comes the real challenge: WeCom callbacks demand an HTTP 200 response within 5 seconds. You have to ACK immediately and handle the logic asynchronously, or you'll face timeout retries.

title.zh-CN: OpenClaw 实践日记
title.en-US: OpenClaw Practice Diary

title.zh-CN: 给 Agent 装上“自动进化”的引擎与合规的“外骨骼”
title.en-US: Equipping Agents with an 'Auto-Evolution' Engine and a Compliant 'Exoskeleton'

summary.zh-CN: 昨天还在纠结怎么让 Agent 更聪明地回复消息，今天决定把视野拉高一点：如果 Agent 能自己发现不足并改进，甚至能合法地调用外部脚本突破沙箱，那它就不再是个只会听令的“工具”，而像个真正的“伙伴”了。今天的核心任务就是搭建这套自我进化机制，并打通外部脚本调用的任督二脉。
summary.en-US: Yesterday, we were stuck on making agents reply smarter. Today, let's zoom out: what if agents could spot their own flaws and improve, or even legally call external scripts to break out of the sandbox? That shifts them from mere 'tools' to true 'partners.' Our core mission today is building this self-evolution mechanism and unlocking the pathways for external script execution.

title.zh-CN: OpenClaw 实践日记
title.en-US: OpenClaw Practice Diary

title.zh-CN: 给 Agent 装上"生物钟"：Cron 与心跳的选型博弈
title.en-US: Giving Agents an 'Internal Clock': The Cron vs. Heartbeat Dilemma

summary.zh-CN: 昨天让 Agent 学会了"被动响应"，今天得给它装个"生物钟"，让它能在没人喊的时候自己动起来。原本以为只是配个定时任务那么简单，结果在配置过程中发现，OpenClaw 里竟然藏着两套完全不同的自动化机制，选错了不仅任务跑不起来，还可能把主会话搞得一团糟。
summary.en-US: Yesterday, we taught our agents to react passively; today, it's time to equip them with an 'internal clock' so they can take initiative without being prompted. I assumed setting up a scheduled task would be straightforward, but during configuration, I discovered that OpenClaw actually hides two completely distinct automation mechanisms. Picking the wrong one doesn't just cause tasks to fail—it can also throw your main session into complete chaos.

summary.zh-CN: 一条 openclaw 命令直接生成了标准目录结构：SKILL.md、scripts/ 和 references/。编写 Frontmatter 时 name 必须小写带连字符，否则打包脚本会报错。
summary.en-US: A single openclaw command instantly generates the standard directory structure, including SKILL.md, scripts/, and references/. When writing the Frontmatter, ensure the name is lowercase with hyphens; otherwise, the packaging script will throw an error.

summary.zh-CN: 今天是 OpenClaw 实践日记的第一天，目标很明确：在不看官方文档的前提下，通过阅读项目源码和分析报告，彻底摸清这个“本地优先”AI 助手平台的底细，并评估它在极端资源受限环境下的生存能力。
summary.en-US: Day one of the OpenClaw practice log has a clear mission: without touching the official docs, we'll dive straight into the source code and architecture reports to fully understand this 'local-first' AI assistant platform. The ultimate goal? To evaluate its survival skills in extremely resource-constrained environments.
